data.title: Cipher Mining announces pricing of upsized private offering of $1.1B of senior notes
data.description:
• Cipher Mining (NASDAQ:CIFR) announced on Thursday the pricing of its offering of $1.1B aggregate principal amount of 0.00% convertible senior notes due 2031 in a private offering. 
  • The offering size was increased from the previously announced offering size of $800M aggregate principal amount of notes. Cipher granted the initial purchasers of the notes a 13-day option to purchase up to an additional $200M aggregate principal amount of notes.
  • The notes will be senior, unsecured obligations of Cipher. The notes will not bear regular interest, and the principal amount of the notes will not accrete. The notes will mature on October 1, 2031, unless earlier repurchased, redeemed, or converted. 
  • Cipher estimates that the net proceeds to it from the offering will be approximately $1.08B, after deducting the initial purchasers’ discounts and commissions but before deducting Cipher’s estimated offering expenses.
  • Cipher intends to use approximately $70M of the net proceeds from the offering to fund the cost of entering into the capped call transactions and the remainder to finance a portion of the company's data center construction at the Barber Lake facility.

data.title: Friday's Economic Calendar
data.description:
8:30 AM Personal Income and Outlays 
 
Personal income represents the income that households receive from all sources, including wages and salaries, fringe benefits such as employer contributions to private pension plans, proprietors' income, income from rent, dividends, and interest, and transfer payments such as Social Security and unemployment compensation. 
 
On the month, personal income is expected to be up 0.3 percent, with nominal spending up a moderate 0.4 percent, both suggesting consumer resilience. 
 
9:00 AM Fed's Barkin Speech 
 
Richmond Federal Reserve Bank President Thomas Barkin will speak before the Peterson Institute for International Economics.
 
10:00 AM Consumer Sentiment 
 
The University of Michigan's Consumer Survey Center questions households each month on their assessment of current conditions and expectations of future conditions.
 
Forecasters see no revision from the preliminary readings, with the sentiment index unchanged at 55.4 and inflation expectations unrevised at 4.8 percent.
 
1:00 PM Baker Hughes Rig Count 
 
The Baker Hughes North American rig count tracks weekly changes in the number of active operating oil & gas rigs. 
 
1:00 PM Fed's Bowman Speech 
 
Federal Reserve Vice Chair for Supervision Michelle Bowman will participate in "Approach to Monetary Policy Decision-Making" conversation hosted by the Forecasters' Club of New York.

data.title: Costco plans 35 new warehouses in fiscal 2026 as member upgrades and digital sales accelerate
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Costco Wholesale Corporation (COST) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO Ron Vachris highlighted the opening of 10 new warehouses in Q4, bringing the total to 914 worldwide, and announced plans to open another 35 warehouses in fiscal 2026, including 5 relocations. He stated, "We continue to see significant opportunities for expansion both domestically and internationally across the markets where we currently operate." Net sales for the fiscal year were reported at just under $270 billion, with e-commerce sales exceeding $19.6 billion and a record year for gas volumes.
  • Vachris noted recent milestones, including the full rollout of Coca-Cola in the $1.50 hotdog combo across all food courts and the 30th anniversary of Kirkland Signature, with increased sales penetration helping to offset inflationary pressures from tariffs. He also emphasized the addition of executive member exclusive hours and a $10 Instacart credit, which have led to a meaningful increase in upgrades from Gold Star to executive membership.
  • Vachris detailed technology advancements, such as enhanced checkout technology and improvements in digital and e-commerce, including data augmentation and passwordless sign-in.
  • CFO Gary Millerchip reported, "Net income for the fourth quarter came in at $2.61 billion, or $5.87 per diluted share, up 11% from $2.35 billion or $5.29 per diluted share in the fourth quarter last year." He added, "Net sales for the fourth quarter were $84.43 billion, an increase of 8% from $78.8 billion in the fourth quarter last year." Millerchip highlighted a 14% year-over-year increase in membership fee income to $1.72 billion and a 9.3% rise in paid executive memberships.

Outlook

  • Vachris stated, "We remain confident in our ability to grow market share by continuing to deliver exciting, high-quality items at the best value for our members," despite macroeconomic uncertainty. Millerchip noted the company plans to open 35 warehouses in fiscal 2026 and continues to prioritize investments in technology, remodels, and manufacturing facilities to support growth. He detailed the shift in e-commerce reporting to digitally enabled comparable sales, stating, "For fiscal year 2025, our digitally enabled sales totaled more than $27 billion."
  • Management expects a small decline in renewal rates as online sign-ups, who renew at a slightly lower rate, become a larger part of the membership base, but targets improvement through auto-renewal and targeted digital communications.

Financial Results

  • Q4 net income was $2.61 billion, or $5.87 per diluted share, up from $2.35 billion or $5.29 per share last year. Net sales for the quarter reached $84.43 billion, an 8% increase year-over-year. Comparable sales grew 5.7% (6.4% adjusted), with e-commerce comparable sales up 13.6%. Membership fee income rose by $212 million to $1.72 billion. Executive members reached 38.7 million, representing 47.7% of paid members and 74.2% of sales.
  • The gross margin rate increased to 11.13%. Core-on-core margins improved by 29 basis points, with fresh foods, sundries, and nonfoods all higher year-over-year. Supply chain improvements and increased Kirkland Signature penetration contributed to margin gains. SG&A rate was 9.21%, with wage investments and general liability charges partially offset by productivity gains. Capex for Q4 was approximately $1.97 billion.
  • Fresh sales were up high single digits, led by double-digit growth in meat. Nonfood and food/sundries also posted strong comps, while ancillary businesses like pharmacy and optical saw solid growth. Gas comps were negative due to lower prices, despite higher volumes.

Q&A

  • Christopher Horvers, JPMorgan, asked about extended member hours and their impact. Vachris responded that communication with members has been strong, and the 1% comp lift is based on business analysis. Millerchip noted the impact will unfold further over time.
  • Michael Lasser, UBS, questioned the sustainability of membership renewal rates. Millerchip explained the decline is tied to more online sign-ups, which renew at lower rates, but overall membership income and upgrades remain strong. Management aims to improve renewal through engagement and auto-renewal.
  • Charles Grom, Gordon Haskett, inquired about core-on-core margins and holiday assortment. Millerchip attributed margin gains to broad-based improvements and supply chain efficiency. Vachris highlighted the introduction of new product categories for the holiday season due to space freed up by thinning traditional assortments.
  • Zhihan Ma, Bernstein, asked about the sustainability of membership fee growth. Millerchip expressed continued optimism, citing new warehouse openings and younger member engagement as growth drivers.
  • Simeon Gutman, Morgan Stanley, discussed e-commerce and grocery competition. Vachris said Instacart and Uber business continues to grow and the company remains vigilant about new entrants.
  • Other questions covered unit growth sustainability, CapEx plans, inflation trends, credit card programs, tariffs, and digital roadmap priorities, with management emphasizing ongoing investments and adaptability.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts focused on membership renewal trends, inflation, tariffs, and margin sustainability, with a neutral to slightly positive sentiment, probing for risks but recognizing management's proactive strategies.
  • Management maintained a confident and upbeat tone in prepared remarks and Q&A, emphasizing adaptability and investment in growth initiatives. Phrases like "we feel very good" and "we are confident" were common. During Q&A, management was candid about renewal rate challenges but stressed overall membership and income growth.
  • Compared to last quarter, management's tone remained confident, while analysts maintained a similar level of scrutiny regarding margins, tariffs, and digital strategy.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Expansion plans increased from 27 new warehouses in fiscal 2025 to 35 planned for 2026. E-commerce and digitally enabled sales growth continues to be a focus, with a shift in reporting methodology. Membership fee income growth accelerated, and executive membership upgrades rose following new benefits. Renewal rates declined slightly, consistent with prior comments about digital sign-ups. Gross margin and core-on-core margin improvement trends persisted, supported by increased Kirkland Signature penetration and supply chain efficiencies. Analysts maintained focus on inflation, tariffs, and digital engagement, while management's confidence in navigating challenges and expanding strategically remained steady.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited ongoing macroeconomic uncertainty, potential impacts from tariffs, and the risk of further decline in renewal rates as online sign-ups increase. Millerchip noted, "We would, therefore, expect to continue to see a small decline in our renewal rate as this change in membership mix gets fully reflected." Mitigation strategies include enhancing auto-renewal, targeted communications, supply chain adjustments, and product assortment changes to offset tariff costs. Analysts questioned the sustainability of fee income growth and the impact of new digital members on renewal rates.

Final Takeaway

Costco signaled continued confidence in its growth trajectory, underpinned by an ambitious warehouse expansion plan, strong executive membership trends, and investments in digital and operational capabilities. Management acknowledged near-term headwinds from membership renewal mix and tariffs but emphasized ongoing strategies to drive market share gains, enhance member value, and adapt to changing consumer behavior through technology and product innovation.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: LightPath outlines $90M backlog and accelerated growth as transformation strategy drives record orders
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: LightPath Technologies (LPTH) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO Sam Rubin described LightPath's transformation and the emergence of tangible results, stating, "Over the last few years, we've been working on the transformation of the company, and we're now beginning to see the tangible results of these efforts." Rubin emphasized a strategic pivot from a component manufacturer reliant on low-cost China-based production to a focus on proprietary technology and value-added subsystems, notably in infrared imaging.

  • Rubin highlighted the impact of proprietary Black Diamond glass and the acquisition of G5 Infrared, saying, "The combination of LightPath's materials and our optics, together with, for example, the recently acquired subsidiary of G5 Infrared...is case in point for this." He also noted, "Our backlog today is around $90 million, 9-0 million. That is more than 4x what the backlog was just a few months ago. And with more than 2/3 of this backlog in systems and subsystems, it is clear that the strategy is working."

  • The CEO discussed two recent large orders totaling over $40 million for infrared cameras to be delivered in 2026 and 2027, linked to border surveillance and counter-UAV systems. Rubin noted substantial growth in demand due to supply chain constraints and geopolitical factors affecting materials like germanium, with LightPath's material being positioned as a key alternative.

  • Rubin pointed to additional growth drivers, including the Navy's SPEIR program, the NGSRI program for Lockheed Martin, and an $8 million investment from Ondas Holdings and Unusual Machines to support drone and thermal imaging manufacturing expansion. He stated, "The $8 million investment received from Ondas and UMAC will go towards expanding these efforts, and I expect these efforts to be very fruitful for all 3 companies and the industry as a whole."

  • CFO Albert Miranda stated, "Revenue for the fourth quarter of fiscal 2025 increased 41.4% to $12.2 million as compared to $8.6 million in the same year ago quarter." Miranda reported a gross profit of $2.7 million and noted operating expenses rose due to the G5 acquisition, product development, marketing, and integration costs.

  • Miranda also highlighted, "Net loss in the fourth quarter of fiscal 2025 totaled $7.1 million or $0.16 per basic and diluted share as compared to $2.4 million or $0.06 per basic and diluted share in the same quarter of the prior fiscal year."

Outlook

  • Rubin stated, "We remain very focused on the transformation of LightPath...we expect to see significant growth continuing and our investments in Black Diamond and other differentiators bearing fruit."

  • Miranda discussed a focus on margin expansion in fiscal 2026, with expectations for continued revenue growth due to investments in manufacturing and a detailed go-to-market strategy targeting high-growth areas.

  • Management indicated, "I expect to see margin expansion as a result" of prior manufacturing investments and highlighted a focus on adjusted EBITDA as a performance measure going forward.

Financial Results

  • Miranda reported, "Revenue for the fourth quarter of fiscal 2025 increased 41.4% to $12.2 million as compared to $8.6 million in the same year ago quarter."

  • Sales of infrared components were $4.9 million, visible components $2.8 million, assemblies and modules $4.2 million, and engineering services $0.3 million. Gross profit was $2.7 million, or 22% of total revenues.

  • Operating expenses rose to $7.2 million, driven by acquisition integration, marketing, and new product development. Net loss for the quarter was $7.1 million, while adjusted EBITDA loss was $1.9 million.

  • As of June 30, 2025, cash and cash equivalents totaled $4.9 million, total debt was $5 million, and backlog stood at $37.4 million, with subsequent growth to $90 million.

Q&A

  • Jaeson Schmidt, Lake Street Capital Markets, LLC: Asked about G5's contribution to the quarter and backlog. Miranda answered, "So it was $4.2 million, Jaeson, was the G5 contribution to revenue." Rubin added, "I'd say 2/3 of the backlog altogether is cameras and assemblies."

  • Glenn Mattson, Ladenburg Thalmann: Inquired about capacity expansion at Visimid and its link to Lockheed contracts. Rubin explained the move to a larger facility supports both NGSRI and other product lines, not just Lockheed work.

  • Mattson also asked about gross margin trends. Miranda replied, "In the quarter, we actually thought the mix was...typical. There's $0.5 million...that's the single largest item."

  • Richard Shannon, Craig-Hallum: Sought clarification on backlog measurement. Miranda explained, "Backlog is a real order from a real customer...we do accept orders that are multiple years," with about 60% shipping in fiscal year 2026.

  • Scott Buck, H.C. Wainwright: Asked about the timeline for redesigning G5's products. Rubin indicated new versions are already driving customer orders, even before production.

  • Brian Kinstlinger, Alliance Global Partners: Queried about EBITDA targets with the current backlog. Miranda stated there would be an uplift in gross margin and EBITDA as revenues increase.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts displayed a positive to slightly optimistic tone, frequently congratulating management on transformation and growth, while probing for clarity on backlog, margin expansion, and run-rate profitability.

  • Management maintained a confident tone in both prepared remarks and responses, with Rubin stating, "I can definitely say now with confidence that we're well at an inflection point and are very pleased with where we are."

  • Compared to the previous quarter, both analysts and management appear more optimistic, with greater focus on backlog expansion and strategic wins. The tone shifted from cautious optimism to pronounced confidence, especially regarding large contracts and growth drivers.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Backlog increased from $27.4 million in Q3 to $90 million in Q4, with more than two-thirds now from systems and subsystems.

  • Revenue rose from $9.2 million to $12.2 million quarter-over-quarter, while gross profit remained at $2.7 million but gross margin declined due to inventory reserve charges.

  • Operating expenses increased sharply, largely from acquisition integration and ramping up for new programs.

  • Analysts shifted focus from the integration of G5 and legacy product lines to the impact of large new contracts and margin expansion.

  • Management’s tone evolved from highlighting potential to emphasizing realized growth and future scale.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited ongoing supply chain risks, particularly related to materials like germanium, and the need for continued investment in proprietary alternatives.

  • Integration of acquisitions and the ability to deliver on large multi-year orders remain areas of focus.

  • Analysts expressed concern about achieving margin and EBITDA targets amid rising expenses and the timeline for profitability improvement.

Final Takeaway

LightPath Technologies management emphasized the company’s transformation, record $90 million backlog, and substantial growth in systems and subsystems, with new multi-year orders and strategic investments positioning the company for further expansion. Despite higher operating expenses and net loss, management is confident in achieving margin improvement, leveraging proprietary technology, and capitalizing on robust demand across defense and commercial markets in fiscal 2026 and beyond.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Concentrix targets Q4 revenue up to $2.55B and full-year EPS of $11.23 as AI integration drives growth
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Concentrix Corporation (CNXC) Q3 2025

Management View

  • CEO Christopher Caldwell highlighted that "we exceeded our revenue guidance once again with solid year-on-year growth across the board" and emphasized Concentrix's strengthening market position, saying, "We are gaining share and securing new wins by combining AI, CX and IT services into a powerful, tightly integrated solution." He underlined that "almost 40% of our new wins this year include our AI technology platforms as part of the solution," and projected that the iX suite will be "accretive as we exit this year."
  • Caldwell addressed profitability, stating, "Margins were below plan in the quarter," but assured, "we have line of sight to modest sequential quarterly margin improvement over the next few quarters."
  • He also welcomed new team members from SAI Digital and reiterated optimism in the current strategy, saying, "I'm optimistic about our strategy as we capitalize on the opportunities we have in front of us today."
  • CFO Andre Valentine reported, "We delivered revenue of approximately $2.48 billion, an increase of 2.6% year-on-year on a constant currency basis and 4% year-on-year as reported." He pointed out that "revenue above our guidance range" was driven by banking, financial services, and insurance clients, which "grew 8% year-on-year."
  • Valentine stated, "Our non-GAAP operating income was $305 million, which was below the guidance range we provided on our last call," attributing the shortfall mainly to "excess capacity" related to tariff-impacted clients and accelerated transformation initiatives. He added, "Adjusted EBITDA in the quarter was $359 million, a margin of 14.5%."
  • The company returned $64 million to shareholders, repurchasing $42 million of common shares and distributing $22 million as dividends. Valentine announced, "our Board has authorized an increase to our quarterly dividend to $0.36 per share."

Outlook

  • Valentine provided Q4 2025 guidance: "Q4 revenue of $2.525 billion to $2.550 billion," and for the full year, "fiscal year 2025 revenue of $9.798 billion to $9.823 billion."
  • Constant currency revenue growth for Q4 is expected to range from 1.5% to 2.5%. Full year 2025 guidance was increased to 1.75% to 2% constant currency revenue growth.
  • Q4 non-GAAP operating income is forecast at $320 million to $330 million; full year at $1.25 billion to $1.26 billion.
  • Q4 non-GAAP EPS is guided to $2.85 to $2.96, and full-year non-GAAP EPS to $11.11 to $11.23.
  • Adjusted free cash flow for the year is projected at $585 million to $610 million.
  • The company will prioritize debt repayment in 2026 while maintaining its dividend and share repurchase program.

Financial Results

  • Revenue for Q3 was $2.48 billion, with banking, financial services, and insurance leading growth at 8% year-on-year. Media and communications grew 7%, and retail, travel, and e-commerce increased 3%. Technology, consumer electronics, and healthcare were flat.
  • Non-GAAP operating income was $305 million. Adjusted EBITDA was $359 million, at a 14.5% margin. Non-GAAP diluted EPS was $2.78. GAAP net income was $88 million, with GAAP diluted EPS at $1.34.
  • Adjusted free cash flow rose to $179 million, up $44 million year-on-year, and year-to-date adjusted free cash flow increased $83 million.
  • At quarter-end, cash and cash equivalents were $350 million and total debt $4.8 billion, resulting in net debt of $4.5 billion.
  • The company repaid the EUR 700 million sellers' note related to the Webhelp combination.

Q&A

  • Lucas Morison, Canaccord Genuity: Asked about margin guidance and excess capacity from tariffs. Caldwell explained that the impact was primarily due to a handful of clients and expected normalization over multiple quarters, clarifying, "We do think it will be a multi-quarter normalization."
  • Morison followed up on the iX suite ramp. Caldwell said, "40% of our new wins have our technology, our platforms integrated into the new wins," and noted stronger traction for Hero over Hello, with Hero being "a typical SaaS model where we're charging per seat."
  • David Koning, Robert W. Baird: Inquired about margin baseline for next year. Caldwell described the margin headwinds as "one-off items" and expects a return to historical margins, while Valentine added, "I think it will take a couple of quarters... to kind of take care of kind of these one-off items."
  • Koning also asked about sequential growth in retail, travel, and e-commerce. Valentine stated, "Those are not one-off things... the deals as they are priced... are at the right margins and should be accretive as we go forward."
  • Vincent Colicchio, Barrington Research: Queried about the consolidation trend and pipeline. Caldwell expressed confidence, "I think we're still in early innings," and said the pipeline is "pretty broad-based across our strategic verticals."
  • Ruplu Bhattacharya, BofA Securities: Asked about investment decisions and ROI for transformation projects. Caldwell responded, "We believe in these long-term relationships through thick and thin because they benefit us," emphasizing that investments are focused on clients with strong partnership potential.
  • Bhattacharya sought updates on iX suite investment and breakeven timeline. Caldwell confirmed, "We absolutely expect to be on track... to be breakeven, modestly accretive at the end of Q4 as we exit on our iX suite of products."
  • Bhattacharya asked about free cash flow and dividend rationale. Valentine replied, "We're still very optimistic that we can drive some increase to free cash flow in 2026," and explained the dividend increase as a reflection of confidence in cash flow generation.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analyst tone was neutral to slightly inquisitive, with questions probing the sustainability of growth, margin recovery, and investment returns. Analysts focused on margin normalization, AI product ramp, and capital allocation.
  • Management maintained a confident but measured tone. Caldwell frequently referenced having "line of sight" to improvements and used phrases such as "we are confident" and "we absolutely expect" in relation to AI suite performance and margin recovery. Both in prepared remarks and responses, the tone was optimistic but acknowledged near-term headwinds.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management’s confidence in revenue momentum remains, but the discussion around margin headwinds is more pronounced. Analyst tone is moderately more probing on risks and execution details this quarter.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for full-year revenue and EPS narrowed and was slightly reduced versus previous quarter, and margin improvement is now projected as a multi-quarter process rather than immediate.
  • Both quarters highlighted AI and integrated solutions as growth drivers, but this quarter placed greater emphasis on AI’s direct contribution to new wins and the accretive impact of the iX suite.
  • Margin shortfalls in Q3 were attributed to excess capacity from tariff-related client impacts and accelerated transformation programs, whereas Q2 margin softness was mainly due to labor retention during client pauses.
  • Analysts’ focus shifted from growth acceleration and AI traction in Q2 to more detailed questions on margin normalization, risk management, and capital deployment in Q3.
  • Management confidence in long-term strategy is consistent, but current quarter language is more cautious around the timing of profitability recovery.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management flagged excess capacity related to a handful of tariff-impacted clients as the main driver of margin underperformance, noting the normalization could take several quarters.
  • Accelerated deployment of transformation and AI solutions resulted in short-term margin dilution but is expected to benefit revenue and client retention longer term.
  • Currency headwinds and additional cybersecurity investments for generative AI impacted year-on-year non-GAAP operating income.
  • Analysts highlighted the risk of prolonged margin recovery and questioned the sustainability of recent vertical growth trends.

Final Takeaway

Concentrix management conveyed that revenue growth outpaced guidance on the strength of AI-driven integrated offerings and continued market share gains, but margin softness stemmed from excess capacity with a small group of clients and upfront transformation investments. The outlook for Q4 and the full year remains positive, with increased revenue guidance, further AI adoption, and a path to sequential margin improvement. Management remains confident in the long-term strategy and expects the iX suite to become accretive as the year ends, while capital returns to shareholders are maintained through an increased dividend and ongoing share repurchases. Investors are encouraged to track the pace of margin normalization and the scaling of AI solutions as key drivers moving forward.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Crude oil ends mixed as Russian supply concerns offset by upwardly revised U.S. GDP
data.description:
Crude futures closed little changed Thursday after rallying to the highest levels since July in the previous session, driven by concerns that Ukraine's attacks on Russia's energy infrastructure could disrupt supplies as well as a surprise decline in U.S. weekly crude inventories.

Oil prices enjoyed added support after Russian Deputy Prime Minister Alexander Novak said the country would introduce a partial ban on diesel exports until the year-end and extend an existing ban on gasoline exports.

"There's no doubt that right now that the Russian hits on their oil infrastructure by Ukraine means that really is Ground Zero," and if Europe carries out tougher sanctions on Russian oil and gas, it "could create a situation where we could start to see significant change in market psychology and a potential breakout to the upside," Price Futures Group's Phil Flynn said in a note.

Offsetting some gains, the Commerce Department reported U.S. gross domestic product increased at an upwardly revised 3.8% annualized rate last quarter, somewhat tempering optimism that the Federal Reserve would further reduce interest rates.

Also, the Kurdistan Regional Government announced the resumption of oil exports after a tripartite agreement among Iraq's oil ministry, the KRG ministry of natural resources and affected companies.

Front-month Nymex crude (CL1:COM) for November delivery lost one cent to $64.98/bbl, while front-month Brent November crude finished +0.1% to $69.42/bbl, its highest settlement value since August 1, and U.S. natural gas (NG1:COM) rose for the third straight session, with the front-month October contract closing +1.6% to $2.904/MMBtu.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:USO), (BNO), (NYSEARCA:UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG), (NYSEARCA:XLE)

data.title: TechnipFMC wins 'substantial' subsea contract from Exxon for Guyana’s Hammerhead project
data.description:
TechnipFMC (NYSE:FTI) +1.6% post-market Thursday after saying it was awarded a "substantial" contract by Exxon Mobil (XOM) to supply subsea production systems for the Hammerhead development in Guyana's Stabroek Block; the company considers a "substantial" contract in the $250 million-to-$500 million range.

TechnipFMC (NYSE:FTI) said it will provide project management, engineering, and manufacturing of subsea production systems supporting both production and water injection capabilities, including products from its Subsea 2.0 platform, including subsea trees, manifolds, and associated controls.

Exxon (XOM) recently made a final investment decision on the $6.8 billion Hammerhead project, which will include 18 production and injection wells, and is expected to begin oil and gas production in 2029.

Hammerhead is TechnipFMC's (FTI) seventh greenfield project award from Exxon (XOM) since the first Stabroek development was sanctioned in 2017.

data.title: CBOT wheat, corn futures gain as U.S. export sales rose more than forecast
data.description:
U.S. wheat and corn futures rose Thursday after the U.S. Department of Agriculture reported weekly export sales that beat analyst estimates.

Wheat (W_1:COM) for December delivery closed +1.4% to $5.27 per bushel on the Chicago Board of Trade, as U.S. wheat sales for the week ended September 18 jumped to 540,000 metric tons from 388,000 tons in previous week, exceeding the analyst consensus of 417,000 tons, according to the USDA's latest report.

Leading wheat buyers this week included the Philippines, Italy, Indonesia, and Japan, while China remained nearly absent as a buyer, which has pressured soybeans.

CBOT corn (C_1:COM) for December delivery settled +0.4% to $4.25 3/4 per bushel, as weekly U.S. sales rose to a higher than expected 1.92 million metric tons from 1.23 million tons in the previous week, the USDA said, while soybeans (S_1:COM) for November delivery ended +0.3% to $10.11 3/4 per bushel.

U.S. grain futures pared earlier gains as irritation rose around China's absence from the U.S. grain export, with American Soybean Association President Caleb Ragland criticizing the move by Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent to extend credit to Argentina, saying it enabled China to again bypass the U.S. for its soy needs.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:WEAT), (NYSEARCA:CORN), (NYSEARCA:SOYB), (DBA), (MOO)

data.title: Mobio Technologies to acquire Karve IT from Plank Ventures
data.description:
• Mobio Technologies (TSXV:MBO:CA) has entered into a binding share purchase agreement to acquire 310,000 of the issued and outstanding common shares of Karve IT from Plank Ventures.
  • Plank will be issued 2.86M common shares in the capital of the company. Concurrently, Tracksuit Movers, a wholly owned subsidiary of Mobio, will transfer all of its investment in Karve consisting of 200,000 common shares in the capital of Karve to Mobio by way of dividend in kind.
  • It is anticipated that upon closing of the Acquisition, Mobio will hold directly 100% of the issued and outstanding shares of Karve, making it the sole direct shareholder of Karve.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Black Hills announces pricing of $450 million debt offering
data.description:
• Black Hills (NYSE:BKH) announced on Thursday the pricing of a registered public debt offering of $450 million aggregate principal amount of 4.550% senior unsecured notes due Jan. 31, 2031.
  • The company expects the offering to close on Oct. 2, 2025.
  • The company intends to use the net proceeds from the offering to repay, redeem or otherwise retire all $300 million aggregate principal amount outstanding of its 3.950% notes due Jan. 15, 2026, at or before maturity. Any remaining net proceeds may be used for general corporate purposes.
  • BKH +0.21% after hours to $60.15.
  • Source: Press Release
